relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The dwelling-place and altar are anointed and made ready.
The tribal leaders step forward together.
They bring wagons and oxen for the work of the sanctuary.
YHWH speaks about the gifts.
Resources are distributed according to actual service.
Moses receives and assigns the transport gifts.
Gershon receives what its burden requires.
Merari receives more because its burden is heavier.
Kohath receives no wagons because the holy things are carried on shoulders.
The leaders begin the dedication of the altar.
One leader comes each day.
Judah comes first through Nahshon.
Judah brings silver vessels full of grain offering.
Judah brings gold and incense.
Judah brings the burnt offering.
Judah brings the purification offering.
Judah brings the peace/well-being offering.
Issachar comes second through Nethanel.
Issachar brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Issachar brings the same gold and incense.
Issachar brings the same burnt offering.
Issachar brings the same purification offering.
Issachar brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Zebulun comes third through Eliab.
Zebulun brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Zebulun brings the same gold and incense.
Zebulun brings the same burnt offering.
Zebulun brings the same purification offering.
Zebulun brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Reuben comes fourth through Elizur.
Reuben brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Reuben brings the same gold and incense.
Reuben brings the same burnt offering.
Reuben brings the same purification offering.
Reuben brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Simeon comes fifth through Shelumiel.
Simeon brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Simeon brings the same gold and incense.
Simeon brings the same burnt offering.
Simeon brings the same purification offering.
Simeon brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Gad comes sixth through Eliasaph.
Gad brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Gad brings the same gold and incense.
Gad brings the same burnt offering.
Gad brings the same purification offering.
Gad brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Ephraim comes seventh through Elishama.
Ephraim brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Ephraim brings the same gold and incense.
Ephraim brings the same burnt offering.
Ephraim brings the same purification offering.
Ephraim brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Manasseh comes eighth through Gamaliel.
Manasseh brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Manasseh brings the same gold and incense.
Manasseh brings the same burnt offering.
Manasseh brings the same purification offering.
Manasseh brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Benjamin comes ninth through Abidan.
Benjamin brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Benjamin brings the same gold and incense.
Benjamin brings the same burnt offering.
Benjamin brings the same purification offering.
Benjamin brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Dan comes tenth through Ahiezer.
Dan brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Dan brings the same gold and incense.
Dan brings the same burnt offering.
Dan brings the same purification offering.
Dan brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Asher comes eleventh through Pagiel.
Asher brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Asher brings the same gold and incense.
Asher brings the same burnt offering.
Asher brings the same purification offering.
Asher brings the same peace/well-being offering.
Naphtali comes twelfth through Ahira.
Naphtali brings the same silver vessels and grain offering.
Naphtali brings the same gold and incense.
Naphtali brings the same burnt offering.
Naphtali brings the same purification offering.
Naphtali brings the same peace/well-being offering.
The twelve equal gifts become one dedication.
The silver total is remembered.
The gold and incense total is remembered.
The burnt and purification offerings are totaled.
The peace/well-being offerings are totaled.
At the end of all the giving, Moses hears the voice from above the ark.

contemplative_note: Numbers 7 turns repetition into equality. Every tribe is named, every gift is honored, every offering is the same, and the chapter ends not in donor prestige but in the voice of YHWH speaking from the center.
